notes: RANCH's 2003 sequel to Kwik Stop Manager · the in-fiction 'official' Sam.  Set at the Hi-Way 30 Stop-N-Go outside Clatskanie, the summer after KSM.  Twelve weeks · a mid-summer armed-robbery beat · three endings.

shelf.label_subtitle: twelve weeks · one register · keep your head
shelf.shelf_condition: played once through · a receipt from an actual convenience store used as a bookmark in the manual
shelf.provenance_note: Olaf pre-ordered it the day RANCH announced a sequel.  He finished it in nine days and then stood in the kitchen for a while.
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: RANCH's follow-up shipped eight months before Oneironautics' Estuary 3 · whose Kwik Stop half was the studio's failed Sam's Summer Shifts competitor.  The two ex-Oneironautics designers on RANCH's staff named the protagonist Sam as a specific friendly needle at their old studio.  Oneironautics named THEIR Estuary 3 clerk Sam right back.  Both studios denied everything in interviews.  Everyone knew.
shelf.meta_note: The rivalry is the joke: RANCH's official Sam versus Oneironautics' Sam.  Slowstick Review Quarterly ran the two box shots side by side under the headline 'SAM VS SAM'.

acts_deferred_note: Playable v1 shipped as a twelve-week beat-sim · full shift-floor simulation deferred.
